devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । पितृभ्यां प्रस्थिते साध्वी पतिं इङ्कितकोविदा । नित्यं पर्यचरत् प्रीत्या भवानीव भवं प्रभुम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca pitṛbhyāṃ prasthite sādhvī patiṃ iṅkitakovidā nityaṃ paryacarat prītyā bhavānīva bhavaṃ prabhum

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: When the parents of Devahūti departed, the pious lady who was expert in reading the inward thoughts of her husband, always attended upon him affectionately as the goddess Pārvatī did for Śiva, the Lord of the world.

devanagari: विश्रम्भेणात्मशौचेन गौरवेण दमेन च । शुश्रूषया सौहृदेन वाचा मधुरया च भोः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: viśrambheṇātmaśaucena gauraveṇa damena ca śuśrūṣayā sauhṛdena vācā madhurayā ca bhoḥ
translation: Having given up passion, fraud, hatred, avarice, objectionable behaviour and pride, she who was very alert and was always ready (to serve Kardama), gave satisfaction to her brilliant husband by her confidence (in him), her physical and mental purity, respectfulness, self-control, service, sincere affection and sweet speech.

devanagari: विसृज्य कामं दम्भं च द्वेषं लोभमघं मदम् । अप्रमत्तोद्यता नित्यं तेजीयांसमतोषयत् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: visṛjya kāmaṃ dambhaṃ ca dveṣaṃ lobhamaghaṃ madam apramattodyatā nityaṃ tejīyāṃsamatoṣayat
translation: Having given up passion, fraud, hatred, avarice, objectionable behaviour and pride, she who was very alert and was always ready (to serve Kardama), gave satisfaction to her brilliant husband by her confidence (in him), her physical and mental purity, respectfulness, self-control, service, sincere affection and sweet speech.

devanagari: स वै देवर्षिवर्यस्तां मानवीं समनुव्रताम् । दैवाद्‍गरीयसः पत्युः आशासानां महाशिषः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa vai devarṣivaryastāṃ mānavīṃ samanuvratām daivād‍garīyasaḥ patyuḥ āśāsānāṃ mahāśiṣaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: The eminent divine sage was verily overwhelmed with affection (favour) for that daughter of Manu and spoke in words choked with intense love to Devahūti—Devahūti who was devoutly attached to him and was expecting great blessings from her husband whom she considered more powerful than destiny (or a deity). She was greatly emaciated and weak by rigid observance of the vow (of serving her husband) for a long period.

devanagari: कालेन भूयसा क्षामां कर्शितां व्रतचर्यया । प्रेमगद्‍गदया वाचा पीडितः कृपयाब्रवीत् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: kālena bhūyasā kṣāmāṃ karśitāṃ vratacaryayā premagad‍gadayā vācā pīḍitaḥ kṛpayābravīt
translation: The eminent divine sage was verily overwhelmed with affection (favour) for that daughter of Manu and spoke in words choked with intense love to Devahūti—Devahūti who was devoutly attached to him and was expecting great blessings from her husband whom she considered more powerful than destiny (or a deity). She was greatly emaciated and weak by rigid observance of the vow (of serving her husband) for a long period.

devanagari: कर्दम उवाच । तुष्टोऽहमद्य तव मानवि मानदायाः । शुश्रूषया परमया परया च भक्त्या । यो देहिनामयमतीव सुहृत्स्वदेहो । नावेक्षितः समुचितः क्षपित...
english_devnagari: kardama uvāca tuṣṭo śuśrūṣayā paramayā parayā ca bhaktyā yo dehināmayamatīva suhṛtsvadeho nāvekṣitaḥ samucitaḥ kṣapituṃ madarthe

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Kardama said:
“Oh daughter of Manu, I am today highly pleased by your respectful, excellent service and intense devotion. To every being possessing a body, one’s own person is extremely dear and worthy of being cared for. You, however, have not spared it (your body) as you wasted it over for me (in my service).

devanagari: ये मे स्वधर्मनिरतस्य तपःसमाधि । विद्यात्मयोगविजिता भगवत्प्रसादाः । तानेव ते मदनुसेवनयावरुद्धान् । दृष्टिं प्रपश्य वितराम्यभयानशोकान् ॥ ७...
english_devnagari: ye me svadharmaniratasya tapaḥsamādhi vidyātmayogavijitā bhagavatprasādāḥ tāneva te madanusevanayāvaruddhān dṛṣṭiṃ prapaśya vitarāmyabhayānaśokān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ ji class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 9 verb]

[nominative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ ji class 1 verb], [nominative plural from √ ji class 9 verb], [vocative plural from √ ji class 9 verb], [accusative plural from √ ji class 9 verb]
[nominative single from √ mad class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ mad class 1 verb]

translation: Just have a look at the blessings of the Almighty Lord secured by me who have been thoroughly devoted to my path of righteousness—blessings secured by the dint of my penance, meditation, Vidyā, and concentration of mind. These blessings which transcend fear and sorrow, have been achieved by you by your service to me. I give you the divine insight.

devanagari: अन्ये पुनर्भगवतो भ्रुव उद्विजृम्भ । विभ्रंशितार्थरचनाः किमुरुक्रमस्य । सिद्धासि भुङ्क्ष्व विभवान् निजधर्मदोहान् । दिव्यान् नरैर्दुरधिगान...
english_devnagari: anye punarbhagavato bhruva udvijṛmbha vibhraṃśitārtharacanāḥ kimurukramasya siddhāsi bhuṅkṣva vibhavān nijadharmadohān divyān narairduradhigān nṛpavikriyābhiḥ
translation: Of what merit are other pleasures, the desires (expectations) about which are foiled by the (slightest frowning) bend of the eyebrow of Lord Viṣṇu of immense powers? You have achieved your object. Enjoy the rich heavenly pleasures which accrue to you by your observance of the path of virtue—pleasures unattainable even to kings who covet for them.”[1]
purport:
Footnote [1]:
nṛpa-vikriyābhiḥ: (i) Men with the perverted notions of themselves being kings.—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā
(ii) By kings who perform Aśvamedha and other sacriñces specially prescribed for them—Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa

devanagari: एवं ब्रुवाणमबलाखिलयोगमाया । विद्याविचक्षणमवेक्ष्य गताधिरासीत् । सम्प्रश्रयप्रणयविह्वलया गिरेषद् । व्रीडावलोकविलसद् हसिताननाह ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ bruvāṇamabalākhilayogamāyā vidyāvicakṣaṇamavekṣya gatādhirāsīt sampraśrayapraṇayavihvalayā gireṣad vrīḍāvalokavilasad hasitānanāha

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: Realising that Kardama who spoke thus was proficient in all yoga-māyā and Vidyās, the lady (Devahūti) became free from anxieties. With her face beaming with smiles and slightly bashful looks, she spoke in words faltering with love and modesty.

devanagari: देवहूतिरुवाच । राद्धं बत द्विजवृषैतदमोघयोग । मायाधिपे त्वयि विभो तदवैमि भर्तः । यस्तेऽभ्यधायि समयः सकृदङ्गसङ्गो । भूयाद्‍गरीयसि गुणः प्...
english_devnagari: devahūtiruvāca rāddhaṃ bata dvijavṛṣaitadamoghayoga māyādhipe tvayi vibho tadavaimi bhartaḥ yaste bhūyād‍garīyasi guṇaḥ prasavaḥ satīnām

translation:
Devahūti said:
“Oh eminent Brāhmaṇa, I am happy to know that all this (described by you) is within your powers—you who are the master of the unfailing powers of Yoga-māyā, my lord. (I request) that there should be at least one contact with your person which has been promised by you, oh Lord. To beget a child from a great husband (like you) is a blessing to virtuous women.

devanagari: तत्रेतिकृत्यमुपशिक्ष यथोपदेशं । येनैष मे कर्शितोऽतिरिरंसयाऽत्मा । सिद्ध्येत ते कृतमनोभवधर्षिताया । दीनस्तदीश भवनं सदृशं विचक्ष्व ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tatretikṛtyamupaśikṣa yathopadeśaṃ yenaiṣa me karśito siddhyeta te kṛtamanobhavadharṣitāyā dīnastadīśa bhavanaṃ sadṛśaṃ vicakṣva

translation: Oh Lord, for that purpose (be pleased to) arrange for the necessary materials as per prescription (in the Kāma- Śāstra). My body which has been tormented by (sexual) passion provoked by you and which has been emaciated with excessive desire for sexual enjoyment, will thereby become fit for it. Therefore think of a suitable mansion.”

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । प्रियायाः प्रियमन्विच्छन् कर्दमो योगमास्थितः । विमानं कामगं क्षत्तः तर्ह्येवाविरचीकरत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca priyāyāḥ priyamanvicchan kardamo yogamāsthitaḥ vimānaṃ kāmagaṃ kṣattaḥ tarhyevāviracīkarat

translation:
Maitreya said:
Kardama resorted to meditation for fulfilling the desired object of his beloved. He created an aerial mansion capable of going as per the occupant’s will, oh Vidura.

devanagari: सर्वकामदुघं दिव्यं सर्वरत्‍नसमन्वितम् । सर्वर्द्ध्युपचयोदर्कं मणिस्तम्भैरुपस्कृतम् ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: sarvakāmadughaṃ divyaṃ sarvarat‍nasamanvitam sarvarddhyupacayodarkaṃ maṇistambhairupaskṛtam
translation: It was a heavenly structure yielding all desired objects, decked with all (nine kinds of) costly jewels. (In it) all kinds of riches and prosperity were ever-increasing. It was beautified by columns of precious stones.

devanagari: दिव्योपकरणोपेतं सर्वकालसुखावहम् । पट्टिकाभिः पताकाभिः विचित्राभिः अलङ्कृतम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: divyopakaraṇopetaṃ sarvakālasukhāvaham paṭṭikābhiḥ patākābhiḥ vicitrābhiḥ alaṅkṛtam
translation: (It was) furnished with heavenly articles (like furniture and utensils). It was pleasant in all seasons. It was decorated with various kinds of silken buntings and flags.

devanagari: स्रग्भिर्विचित्रमाल्याभिः मञ्जुशिञ्जत् षडङ्घ्रिभिः । दुकूलक्षौमकौशेयैः नानावस्त्रैः विराजितम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: sragbhirvicitramālyābhiḥ mañjuśiñjat ṣaḍaṅghribhiḥ dukūlakṣaumakauśeyaiḥ nānāvastraiḥ virājitam

translation: (It was beautified) with garlands of flowers of variegated colours with swarms of sweetly humming bees hovering around them, and with fine cotton and silken clothes.

devanagari: उपर्युपरि विन्यस्त निलयेषु पृथक्पृथक् । क्षिप्तैः कशिपुभिः कान्तं पर्यङ्कव्यजनासनैः ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: uparyupari vinyasta nilayeṣu pṛthakpṛthak kṣiptaiḥ kaśipubhiḥ kāntaṃ paryaṅkavyajanāsanaiḥ

translation: (It appeared beautiful as) it was furnished with separate beds, cots, fans and seats in each of the storeys constructed one above the other.

devanagari: तत्र तत्र विनिक्षिप्त नानाशिल्पोपशोभितम् । महामरकतस्थल्या जुष्टं विद्रुमवेदिभिः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: tatra tatra vinikṣipta nānāśilpopaśobhitam mahāmarakatasthalyā juṣṭaṃ vidrumavedibhiḥ

translation: It appeared very attractive on account of the various works of art arranged and exhibited at different places and with its emerald floors and daises of coral.

devanagari: द्वाःसु विद्रुमदेहल्या भातं वज्रकपाटवत् । शिखरेषु इन्द्रनीलेषु हेमकुम्भैः अधिश्रितम् ॥ १८ ॥

translation: It shone with its doors of red coral, thresholds and panels of diamonds. On its tops of blue sapphires were set gold pitchers.

devanagari: चक्षुष्मत् पद्मरागाग्र्यैः वज्रभित्तिषु निर्मितैः । जुष्टं विचित्रवैतानैः महार्हैः हेमतोरणैः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: cakṣuṣmat padmarāgāgryaiḥ vajrabhittiṣu nirmitaiḥ juṣṭaṃ vicitravaitānaiḥ mahārhaiḥ hematoraṇaiḥ

translation: The excellent rubies set in its walls of diamonds appeared like eyes of the aerial car. It was also furnished with wonderful canopies of variegated colours and costly arches of gold.

devanagari: हंसपारावतव्रातैः तत्र तत्र निकूजितम् । कृत्रिमान् मन्यमानैः स्वान् अधिरुह्याधिरुह्य च ॥ २० ॥

[instrumental plural from √ man class 4 verb], [instrumental plural from √ man class 8 verb]

translation: At various places it was filled with the warbling and cooing of swans and pigeons which mistook artificial birds as real ones like themselves and flew to them.

devanagari: विहारस्थानविश्राम संवेशप्राङ्गणाजिरैः । यथोपजोषं रचितैः विस्मापनम् इवात्मनः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: vihārasthānaviśrāma saṃveśaprāṅgaṇājiraiḥ yathopajoṣaṃ racitaiḥ vismāpanam ivātmanaḥ
translation: It was provided with play-grounds, sleeping apartments, places for enjoyment, quadrangles and outer yards constructed for enjoyment at will, so much so that it appeared wonderful to (Kardama) its maker.

devanagari: ईदृग्गृहं तत्पश्यन्तीं नातिप्रीतेन चेतसा । सर्वभूताशयाभिज्ञः प्रावोचत् कर्दमः स्वयम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: īdṛggṛhaṃ tatpaśyantīṃ nātiprītena cetasā sarvabhūtāśayābhijñaḥ prāvocat kardamaḥ svayam
translation: Kardama who knew the inner thoughts of all beings spoke of his own accord to Devahūti who was not much pleased at heart to look at that type of mansion.

devanagari: निमज्ज्यास्मिन् ह्रदे भीरु विमानं इदमारुह । इदं शुक्लकृतं तीर्थं आशिषां यापकं नृणाम् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: nimajjyāsmin hrade bhīru vimānaṃ idamāruha idaṃ śuklakṛtaṃ tīrthaṃ āśiṣāṃ yāpakaṃ nṛṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: “Oh timid lady, take bath in this pool of water and get in this heavenly mansion. This sacred pool which is created by Viṣṇu, blesses men with all desired boons (objects).”

devanagari: सा तद्‍भर्तुः समादाय वचः कुवलयेक्षणा । सरजं बिभ्रती वासो वेणीभूतांश्च मूर्धजान् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: sā tad‍bhartuḥ samādāya vacaḥ kuvalayekṣaṇā sarajaṃ bibhratī vāso veṇībhūtāṃśca mūrdhajān
translation: In compliance of the order of her husband the lotus-eyed lady who wore a dirty garment and had the hair on head tangled and her body covered with dust and discoloured breasts, entered the sacred waters of the pool in the Sarasvatī (wherein lived auspicious acquatic animals).

devanagari: अङ्गं च मलपङ्केन सञ्छन्नं शबलस्तनम् । आविवेश सरस्वत्याः सरः शिवजलाशयम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: aṅgaṃ ca malapaṅkena sañchannaṃ śabalastanam āviveśa sarasvatyāḥ saraḥ śivajalāśayam
translation: In compliance of the order of her husband the lotus-eyed lady who wore a dirty garment and had the hair on head tangled and her body covered with dust and discoloured breasts, entered the sacred waters of the pool in the Sarasvatī (wherein lived auspicious acquatic animals).

devanagari: सान्तः सरसि वेश्मस्थाः शतानि दश कन्यकाः । सर्वाः किशोरवयसो ददर्शोत्पलगन्धयः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: sāntaḥ sarasi veśmasthāḥ śatāni daśa kanyakāḥ sarvāḥ kiśoravayaso dadarśotpalagandhayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: (When she took a plunge) under the waters of the pool she saw in a house a thousand maids, all of youthful age and fragrant like lotus.

devanagari: तां दृष्ट्वा सहसोत्थाय प्रोचुः प्राञ्जलयः स्त्रियः । वयं कर्मकरीस्तुभ्यं शाधि नः करवाम किम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ dṛṣṭvā sahasotthāya procuḥ prāñjalayaḥ striyaḥ vayaṃ karmakarīstubhyaṃ śādhi naḥ karavāma kim
translation: Seeing her, those girls at once stood up and respectfully folding their hands said, “We are your servants. Please order us what we should do for you.”

devanagari: स्नानेन तां महार्हेण स्नापयित्वा मनस्विनीम् । दुकूले निर्मले नूत्‍ने ददुरस्यै च मानदाः ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: snānena tāṃ mahārheṇa snāpayitvā manasvinīm dukūle nirmale nūt‍ne dadurasyai ca mānadāḥ
translation: With costly materials necessary for bath, the respectful damsels made her take bath and gave her two new clean silken garments to wear.

devanagari: भूषणानि परार्ध्यानि वरीयांसि द्युमन्ति च । अन्नं सर्वगुणोपेतं पानं चैवामृतासवम् ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: bhūṣaṇāni parārdhyāni varīyāṃsi dyumanti ca annaṃ sarvaguṇopetaṃ pānaṃ caivāmṛtāsavam
translation: (They gave her) very valuable brilliant ornaments according to her liking; they served her food of all excellent qualities and nectar-like (sweet and) stimulant drink.

devanagari: अथादर्शे स्वमात्मानं स्रग्विणं विरजाम्बरम् । विरजं कृतस्वस्त्ययनं कन्याभिर्बहुमानितम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: athādarśe svamātmānaṃ sragviṇaṃ virajāmbaram virajaṃ kṛtasvastyayanaṃ kanyābhirbahumānitam
translation: In the mirror she saw herself wearing a garland of flowers, dressed in fine garments, her body very clean and decorated with auspicious marks and greatly respected by the girl attendants.

devanagari: स्नातं कृतशिरःस्नानं सर्वाभरणभूषितम् । निष्कग्रीवं वलयिनं कूजत् काञ्चननूपुरम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: snātaṃ kṛtaśiraḥsnānaṃ sarvābharaṇabhūṣitam niṣkagrīvaṃ valayinaṃ kūjat kāñcananūpuram

translation: (She saw herself) bathed and washed from head (to foot), beautified with all kinds of ornaments, wearing gold pendants round her necks and gold bangles (on her hands) and with tinkling gold ornaments.

devanagari: श्रोण्योरध्यस्तया काञ्च्या काञ्चन्या बहुरत्‍नया । हारेण च महार्हेण रुचकेन च भूषितम् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: śroṇyoradhyastayā kāñcyā kāñcanyā bahurat‍nayā hāreṇa ca mahārheṇa rucakena ca bhūṣitam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ bhūṣ ], [accusative single from √ bhūṣ ]

translation: (She put on) gold girdle studded with many jewels around her hips, and with very costly necklace of pearls and was decorated with auspicious (marks and designs drawn in) saffron etc.

devanagari: सुदता सुभ्रुवा श्लक्ष्ण स्निग्धापाङ्गेन चक्षुषा । पद्मकोशस्पृधा नीलैः अलकैश्च लसन्मुखम् ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: sudatā subhruvā ślakṣṇa snigdhāpāṅgena cakṣuṣā padmakośaspṛdhā nīlaiḥ alakaiśca lasanmukham

translation: Devahūti whose face looked very beautiful by her excellent rows of teeth, well-shaped eyebrows, with her beautiful affectionate side glances of eyes which rivalled with lotus (in beauty) and with her dark blue hair (dangling on her forehead).

devanagari: यदा सस्मार ऋषभं ऋषीणां दयितं पतिम् । तत्र चास्ते सह स्त्रीभिः यत्रास्ते स प्रजापतिः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: yadā sasmāra ṛṣabhaṃ ṛṣīṇāṃ dayitaṃ patim tatra cāste saha strībhiḥ yatrāste sa prajāpatiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: When she remembered her beloved husband, the foremost among the sages, she found herself there with a thousand girls where Prajāpati Kardama was sitting.

devanagari: भर्तुः पुरस्तादात्मानं स्त्रीसहस्रवृतं तदा । निशाम्य तद्योगगतिं संशयं प्रत्यपद्यत ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhartuḥ purastādātmānaṃ strīsahasravṛtaṃ tadā niśāmya tadyogagatiṃ saṃśayaṃ pratyapadyata
translation: Seeing that she was in front of her husband and surrounded by thousand maids, and seeing the power of his Yoga, she was confused with wonder (doubted what it all meant).

devanagari: स तां कृतमलस्नानां विभ्राजन्तीमपूर्ववत् । आत्मनो बिभ्रतीं रूपं संवीतरुचिरस्तनीम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa tāṃ kṛtamalasnānāṃ vibhrājantīmapūrvavat ātmano bibhratīṃ rūpaṃ saṃvītarucirastanīm

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [accusative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

[nominative dual], [nominative plural], [vocative dual], [vocative plural], [accusative dual], [accusative plural], [locative single]

translation: Oh Vidura, the sage in whom love for Devahūti was aroused made her ascend in the aerial car (Vimāna)—Devahūti who washed herself clean in the bath and became resplendent as a new person and appeared in her original pre-marriage beauty with her attractive breasts covered (and hence concealed from view)[2].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
v.l.—stanam—adj. qualifying rūpam [rūpa]: ‘beauty of the charming breasts which were covered etc.

devanagari: विद्याधरीसहस्रेण सेव्यमानां सुवाससम् । जातभावो विमानं तद् आरोहयद् अमित्रहन् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: vidyādharīsahasreṇa sevyamānāṃ suvāsasam jātabhāvo vimānaṃ tad ārohayad amitrahan

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: Oh Vidura, the sage in whom love for Devahūti was aroused made her ascend in the aerial car (Vimāna)—Devahūti who washed herself clean in the bath and became resplendent as a new person and appeared in her original pre-marriage beauty with her attractive breasts covered (and hence concealed from view)[2].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
v.l.—stanam—adj. qualifying rūpam [rūpa]: ‘beauty of the charming breasts which were covered etc.

devanagari: तस्मिन् अलुप्तमहिमा प्रिययानुरक्तो । विद्याधरीभिरुपचीर्णवपुर्विमाने । बभ्राज उत्कचकुमुद्‍गणवानपीच्यः । ताराभिरावृत इव उडुपतिः नभःस्थः ॥...
english_devnagari: tasmin aluptamahimā priyayānurakto vidyādharībhirupacīrṇavapurvimāne babhrāja utkacakumud‍gaṇavānapīcyaḥ tārābhirāvṛta iva uḍupatiḥ nabhaḥsthaḥ
translation: In that aerial mansion, he whose greatness (or freedom) was not diminished and who was loved by his wife and whose person was attended upon by Vidyādhara damsels, shone like the extremely beautiful moon surrounded by stars and with full blown night lotuses around him in the sky[3].
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Bhāvāratha Dīpikā brings out full comparison as follows: Sage Kardama=the full moon; Spacious aerial mansion = the sky; beautiful maiḍs around him the stars; fully bloomed night-lotus = the lotus-like eyes of the maids.
utkaca...ganavān—surrounded by a bevy of maiḍs of beautiful hair: who gave pleasure—Subodhinī Padaratnāvalī gives a more erotic explanation.

devanagari: तेनाष्टलोकपविहारकुलाचलेन्द्र । द्रोणीष्वनङ्गसखमारुतसौभगासु । सिद्धैर्नुतो द्युधुनिपातशिवस्वनासु । रेमे चिरं धनदवल्ललनावरूथी ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tenāṣṭalokapavihārakulācalendra droṇīṣvanaṅgasakhamārutasaubhagāsu siddhairnuto dyudhunipātaśivasvanāsu reme ciraṃ dhanadavallalanāvarūthī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]
[nominative single from √ nu class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ nu class 6 verb]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: With that aerial car, Kardama who was praised by Siddhas and was accompanied by a bevy of jewel-like (extremely beautiful) damsels, enjoyed like Kubera for a long time in the valleys of mount Meru, the foremost amongst the Kulaparvatas[4] which are the places of enjoyment for the eight protectors of the world (lokapāla). The valleys of mount Meru were enjoyable on account of gentle breeze, a friend of the god of Love. They were full of the echoes of the cataracts of the heavenly river.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Principal mountains viz., Mahendra, Malaya, Sahya, Śuktimān, Ṛkṣa, Vindhya and Pāriyātra—ASD. 364.

devanagari: वैश्रम्भके सुरसने नन्दने पुष्पभद्रके । मानसे चैत्ररथ्ये च स रेमे रामया रतः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: vaiśrambhake surasane nandane puṣpabhadrake mānase caitrarathye ca sa reme rāmayā rataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: He enjoyed himself with his beautiful wife in celestial gardens like Vaiśrambhaka, Surasana, Nandana, Puṣpabhadraka, Caitrarathya and in the Mānasa lake.

devanagari: भ्राजिष्णुना विमानेन कामगेन महीयसा । वैमानिकानत्यशेत चरन् लोकान् यथानिलः ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: bhrājiṣṇunā vimānena kāmagena mahīyasā vaimānikānatyaśeta caran lokān yathānilaḥ
translation: With his spacious, resplendant aerial car which could move anywhere according to the occupants’ will, he travelled freely all over worlds like a wind, surpassing other gods.

devanagari: किं दुरापादनं तेषां पुंसां उद्दामचेतसाम् । यैराश्रितस्तीर्थपदः चरणो व्यसनात्ययः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: kiṃ durāpādanaṃ teṣāṃ puṃsāṃ uddāmacetasām yairāśritastīrthapadaḥ caraṇo vyasanātyayaḥ
translation: What is difficult to attain for men of boundless mental powers who have resorted to the sacred feet of Hari who destroys the calamity of Saṃsāra.

devanagari: प्रेक्षयित्वा भुवो गोलं पत्‍न्यै यावान् स्वसंस्थया । बह्वाश्चर्यं महायोगी स्वाश्रमाय न्यवर्तत ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: prekṣayitvā bhuvo golaṃ pat‍nyai yāvān svasaṃsthayā bahvāścaryaṃ mahāyogī svāśramāya nyavartata

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]

translation: Having shown to his wife the sphere of the earth full of wonders on account of all the arrangement of continents etc. the great Yogin returned to his hermitage.

devanagari: विभज्य नवधाऽत्मानं मानवीं सुरतोत्सुकाम् । रामां निरमयन् रेमे वर्षपूगान् मुहूर्तवत् ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: vibhajya navadhā rāmāṃ niramayan reme varṣapūgān muhūrtavat

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Dividing himself into nine[5], the sage enjoyed the beautiful daughter of Manu who was eager for sexual happiness. He enjoyed with her for a number of years as if it was but a short period.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
Intending to beget nine children.—Bhāgavata Candrikā

devanagari: तस्मिन् विमान उत्कृष्टां शय्यां रतिकरीं श्रिता । न चाबुध्यत तं कालं पत्यापीच्येन सङ्गता ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin vimāna utkṛṣṭāṃ śayyāṃ ratikarīṃ śritā na cābudhyata taṃ kālaṃ patyāpīcyena saṅgatā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect middle third single], [imperfect passive third single]

translation: In the aerial car, lying with her beautiful hushand on the excellent bed which increased her love and pleasure, she was not aware of the time passed.

devanagari: एवं योगानुभावेन दम्पत्यो रममाणयोः । शतं व्यतीयुः शरदः कामलालसयोर्मनाक् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ yogānubhāvena dampatyo ramamāṇayoḥ śataṃ vyatīyuḥ śaradaḥ kāmalālasayormanāk

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive dual from √ ram class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ ram class 1 verb]
[genitive dual from √ ram class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ ram class 1 verb]
[genitive dual from √ ram class 1 verb], [locative dual from √ ram class 1 verb]
[optative active third plural], [perfect active third plural]

translation: A hundred years rolled away like a moment while the couple who were passionately eager for sexual pleasure were thus enjoying themselves by the force of their Yogic powers.

devanagari: तस्यां आधत्त रेतस्तां भावयन् आत्मनाऽत्मवित् । नोधा विधाय रूपं स्वं सर्वसङ्कल्पविद्विभुः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasyāṃ ādhatta retastāṃ bhāvayan ātmanā nodhā vidhāya rūpaṃ svaṃ sarvasaṅkalpavidvibhuḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active second plural], [imperfect middle third single]
[nominative single from √ bhū ], [vocative single from √ bhū ]

translation: Due to his intense love (for her) he regarded her as his half. He who could read inner wishes of all (and hence knew her desire to have many children) was competent to satisfy them. The sage who realised his own Self[6], divided himself into nine parts and deposited his semen in her.
purport:
Footnote [6]:
VC. and Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa explain that due to his knowledge of the Supreme Self, Kardama was not so much attached to Devahūti as she was to him so intensely. Consequently Devahūti's contribution was the greater at the time of conception. This resulted in the birth of all female children.

devanagari: अतः सा सुषुवे सद्यो देवहूतिः स्त्रियः प्रजाः । सर्वास्ताश्चारुसर्वाङ्ग्यो लोहितोत्पलगन्धयः ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: ataḥ sā suṣuve sadyo devahūtiḥ striyaḥ prajāḥ sarvāstāścārusarvāṅgyo lohitotpalagandhayaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]
[perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Hence Devahūti gave birth to female children immediately on the same day. All of them were beautiful in all their limbs in every respect and they gave out the fragrance of red lotus.

devanagari: पतिं सा प्रव्रजिष्यन्तं तदाऽलक्ष्योशती बहिः । स्मयमाना विक्लवेन हृदयेन विदूयता ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: patiṃ sā pravrajiṣyantaṃ tadā smayamānā viklavena hṛdayena vidūyatā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ vaś class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ vaś class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ vaś class 2 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smi class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smi class 1 verb]
[nominative plural from √ smi class 1 verb], [vocative plural from √ smi class 1 verb], [accusative plural from √ smi class 1 verb]

translation: At that time the beautiful[7] virtuous wife anticipated that her husband was about to renounce the householder’s life (and to become a Saṃnyāsin). Though she was overcome with fear and pain, she smiled outwardly. She was scratching the ground with her foot beautiful with gem-like nails. With her head hung down (in modesty) she spoke soft winning words, controlling frequently her tears.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
uśatī—Desirous of having a son (Padaratnāvalī, Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa).

devanagari: लिखन्त्यधोमुखी भूमिं पदा नखमणिश्रिया । उवाच ललितां वाचं निरुध्याश्रुकलां शनैः ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: likhantyadhomukhī bhūmiṃ padā nakhamaṇiśriyā uvāca lalitāṃ vācaṃ nirudhyāśrukalāṃ śanaiḥ

Though she was overcome with fear and pain, she smiled outwardly. She was scratching the ground with her foot beautiful with gem-like nails. With her head hung down (in modesty) she spoke soft winning words, controlling frequently her tears.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
uśatī—Desirous of having a son (Padaratnāvalī, Bhāvārtha-dīpikā-prakāśa).

devanagari: देवहूतिरुवाच । सर्वं तद्‍भगवान् मह्यं उपोवाह प्रतिश्रुतम् । अथापि मे प्रपन्नाया अभयं दातुमर्हसि ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: devahūtiruvāca sarvaṃ tad‍bhagavān mahyaṃ upovāha pratiśrutam athāpi me prapannāyā abhayaṃ dātumarhasi

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[nominative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ mah class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ mah ], [accusative single from √ mah ]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Devahūti saidL
Your worship has fulfilled every promise that you gave to me. You however should give me protection[8] (against fear from misery of Saṃsāra) as I have resorted to you.
purport:
Footnote [8]:
abhayam [abhaya]—(i) a son to protect me (Padaratnāvalī).
(ii) Forgiveness for the request I am making again (Kramasandarbha.).

devanagari: ब्रह्मन् दुहितृभिस्तुभ्यं विमृग्याः पतयः समाः । कश्चित्स्यान्मे विशोकाय त्वयि प्रव्रजिते वनम् ॥ ५२ ॥
english_devnagari: brahman duhitṛbhistubhyaṃ vimṛgyāḥ patayaḥ samāḥ kaścitsyānme viśokāya tvayi pravrajite vanam
translation: Oh Brāhmaṇa, your daughters will themselves have to find out suitable husbands. When you proceed to the forest as a recluse, there should be someone (a son) to relieve me of my sorrow[9].
purport:
Footnote [9]:
viśokāya—To advise and guide me in real knowledge as begetting daughters does not amount to repaying the debt of forefathers, stay till a son is born—Bhāvāratha Dīpikā

devanagari: एतावतालं कालेन व्यतिक्रान्तेन मे प्रभो । इन्द्रियार्थप्रसङ्गेन परित्यक्तपरात्मनः ॥ ५३ ॥
english_devnagari: etāvatālaṃ kālena vyatikrāntena me prabho indriyārthaprasaṅgena parityaktaparātmanaḥ
translation: Oh Lord, I have given up (every thought about) the supreme self. It is enough that I have spent this much time (life) in satisfying the cravings of my senses.

devanagari: इन्द्रियार्थेषु सज्जन्त्या प्रसङ्गस्त्वयि मे कृतः । अजानन्त्या परं भावं तथाप्यस्तु अभयाय मे ॥ ५४ ॥
english_devnagari: indriyārtheṣu sajjantyā prasaṅgastvayi me kṛtaḥ ajānantyā paraṃ bhāvaṃ tathāpyastu abhayāya me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: I who am attached to the objects of senses, have associated myself with you (for that satisfaction) without realizing your higher (and real) Self. However let this association lead to my protection (i.e. liberation from Saṃsāra).

devanagari: सङ्गो यः संसृतेर्हेतुः असत्सु विहितोऽधिया । स एव साधुषु कृतो निःसङ्गत्वाय कल्पते ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: saṅgo yaḥ saṃsṛterhetuḥ asatsu vihito sa eva sādhuṣu kṛto niḥsaṅgatvāya kalpate

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]

translation: The Association with the wicked formed through ignorance is the cause of the Saṃsāra. That very association if formed with the good ones, leads to non-attachment to Saṃsāra.

devanagari: नेह यत्कर्म धर्माय न विरागाय कल्पते । न तीर्थपदसेवायै जीवन्नपि मृतो हि सः ॥ ५६ ॥
english_devnagari: neha yatkarma dharmāya na virāgāya kalpate na tīrthapadasevāyai jīvannapi mṛto hi saḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]
[nominative single from √ jīv class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ jīv class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ mṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ mṛ class 6 verb]

translation: A person whose action does not contribute to the righteous path (dharma) or to non-attachment or to the service (worship) of the sacred feet of Hari, is meant as good as dead though physically alive.

devanagari: साहं भगवतो नूनं वञ्चिता मायया दृढम् । यत्त्वां विमुक्तिदं प्राप्य न मुमुक्षेय बन्धनात् ॥ ५७ ॥
english_devnagari: sāhaṃ bhagavato nūnaṃ vañcitā māyayā dṛḍham yattvāṃ vimuktidaṃ prāpya na mumukṣeya bandhanāt

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ vañc ], [vocative plural from √ vañc ]
[nominative plural from √ vañc ], [vocative plural from √ vañc ], [accusative plural from √ vañc ]

translation:
As a matter of fact, I am completely deceived by the Māyā of the Lord, for, though I have (by marriage) obtained you who can give me liberation, I did not cherish any desire for it (liberation).
